bullets.text: Q3 net income rose 34% to $409.6 million; year-to-date net income up 12% to $1.25 billion; Q3 combined ratio of 87.3 versus 90.4 in Q3 2005 (aided by the absence of 2005's hurricane losses).
Net written premium grew less than 1% in Q3; retention identified as the biggest growth opportunity — management shifted primary focus from new applications to keeping existing customers longer.

Earned premium per auto policy down about 3% year-over-year from rate decreases and mix shifts; management announced intent to lower rates selectively where policy life increase would more than offset margin reduction.
Cancellation rate remained relatively constant but higher than desired; policy expirations, especially from price-sensitive customers finding lower rates, identified as the primary leakage point.

Six additional concierge centers opened in Q3, bringing year-to-date total to 22 and all-time operating sites to 47; Net Promoter Score adopted companywide as the primary customer satisfaction measure.

Homesite Insurance joint marketing agreement signed for Agency and Direct channels, enabling homeowners, renters, and condo insurance quotes; pilot programs begun in both channels to aid multi-product retention.
Investment income up 23% over Q3 2005; no specific share repurchase or capital action disclosed for the quarter beyond continued strong capital position.
